title: The Lost Hotel Room
summary: Emily checks into a mysterious old hotel with a strange room.
coverScene: A young woman stands in a dark, dusty hotel hallway holding a metal key, looking down a long passage under a low wooden ceiling.

sentences.text: The rain was falling heavily when Emily finally arrived at the old, dark hotel that she had booked online.
The lobby was completely silent, and the manager, who did not even look up, slowly handed her a metal key.
According to the paper map which was on the desk, her room, number four hundred and eight, did not exist.
When she asked for directions, the silent man simply pointed toward a long, narrow passage that led into the darkness.
She walked down the cold hall, but the numbers on the wooden doors did not seem to follow any clear order.
If she had known how strange this place was, she would have chosen a different hotel in the city center.
Finally, she found her door at the very end of a dark corner where the roof was extremely low.
The heavy key turned with a loud sound, revealing a dark bedroom that had not been used for decades.
She dropped her bags on the floor, feeling an immediate sense of worry as the door shut tightly behind her.
There were no windows in the room, and the only light came from a small electric light that was covered in dust.
The air was so dry and cold that she could see her own breath as she sat on the bed.
After checking her phone, she realized with growing panic that there was absolutely no service in this deep, quiet room.
She decided to go back to the front desk, but when she opened the door, the passage was completely dark.
It seemed that all the lights in the hall had been turned off by someone while she was inside.
She closed her door again and locked it, hoping that the morning would bring some simple explanation for everything.
As she lay on the hard bed, she listened to the deep silence of the hotel, which felt completely empty.
If there were other guests in this building, she would hear their voices.
Instead, the only sound was the constant beat of her own heart, which seemed to grow louder with every passing minute.
Late at night, a strange noise started to come from the room next door, which was number four hundred and nine.
It sounded like someone was dragging a heavy wooden box across the floor, followed by a slow, wet sliding sound.
Emily stood up and put her ear against the wall, trying to determine what was happening on the other side.
She knew that the room next door should be empty, because the manager had told her she was alone here.
Suddenly, the dragging noise stopped, and a soft whisper seemed to speak directly into her ear from through the wall.
It was a cold voice that repeated her name over and over again, calling her to come closer to the door.
Frightened, she backed away from the wall until her legs hit the edge of the bed and she fell down.
The voice next door began to laugh, a low and horrible sound that did not belong to any human being.
She realized that if she stayed in this room any longer, she would lose her mind completely from the fear.
She grabbed her bags and ran to the door, her hands shaking so much that she almost dropped the key.
When she finally opened it, she saw that the dark passage was no longer empty as it had been before.
Long, thin shadows were moving along the walls, sliding toward her room like cold fingers reaching out of the dark.
She ran in the opposite direction, hoping to find the stairs or a lift that could take her down.
However, every passage she took only led her deeper into the giant hotel, which seemed to grow larger around her.
The doors she passed had no numbers now, and some of them were painted with strange, red symbols.
She could hear soft crying and whispering coming from behind almost every single wooden door that she ran past.
It felt as if the hotel itself was alive, changing its shape to keep her lost inside its walls forever.
If she had checked the map more carefully at the front desk, she might have seen the warning signs.
Suddenly, she saw a bright light at the end of a long hall and ran toward it with all her strength.
She emerged into the main hall, but it was completely different from the place she had entered earlier that evening.
The furniture was covered in thick dust, and the beautiful windows were now blocked by solid wooden boards.
The front desk was empty, and the paper map that she had left there was gone, replaced by a mirror.
When Emily looked into the mirror, she did not see her own face, but rather a dark space behind her.
In that dark space, a tall figure with no face was standing right behind her, holding her metal room key.
She tried to scream, but no sound came from her throat as she turned around to face the dark figure.
There was nothing there, yet she could feel its cold breath on her skin and hear its soft voice whispering.
The voice told her that she was now part of the hotel, just like everyone else who had checked in.
She realized with terrible pain that she would never find the door that led back to the outside world again.
If she had never entered this building, she would still be safe at home in her own warm bed.
Instead, she was trapped in this lost hotel, where the night never ended and there was no way out.
The front door of the hotel was now locked from the outside, and the key was lost forever.
Emily walked slowly back down the long passage toward room four hundred and eight, her new permanent home.
As she walked, her own steps became silent, and her body began to fade into a thin, gray shadow.
She was no longer a guest who was visiting the hotel, but rather a part of its dark history.
When the next guest arrives on a rainy night, they will hear a soft voice whispering through the walls.
It will be Emily, warning them to run away before they also become lost in the hotel forever.

quiz.q: Why was Emily confused when she first looked at the hotel map?
options: It was written in a language she could not understand.
Her room number, four hundred and eight, was not on it.
The map was completely covered in black paint.

quiz.q: What did Emily hear coming from the room next door?
options: Loud music and people laughing at a party.
Someone dragging a heavy box and whispering her name.
A telephone ringing constantly throughout the night.

quiz.q: What happened when Emily looked into the mirror in the main hall?
options: She saw a faceless figure standing behind her.
The mirror broke into many small pieces.
She saw her own face, but she looked much older.

options: She wanted to find the manager.
The manager pointed her toward her room.
Her paper map told her to go there.

Someone knocking on the wooden wall.

options: She escaped the hotel and went home.
She became a gray shadow trapped in the hotel.
She found a key and unlocked the front door.
